deep_dive.title: Antagonists Perturb the MC2R MRAP Complex and Reshape Receptor Conformations
deep_dive.abstract: Melanocortin 2 receptor (MC2R) is a G protein-coupled receptor (GPCR) for adrenocorticotropic hormone (ACTH), and its trafficking and signalling are associated with the melanocortin receptor accessory protein (MRAP). Mutations in either MC2R or MRAP disrupt this signalling and cause familial glucocorticoid deficiency. Here, we combine native mass spectrometry (MS) and hydrogen deuterium exchange mass spectrometry (HDX MS) to uncover how MRAP association and post-translational modification status shape the conformations of MC2R. Using native MS, we demonstrate that MC2R associates with MRAP or when MRAP is depleted the protein is extensively palmitoylated at the C-terminus. ACTH binding is restricted to the MC2R MRAP complex. By contrast antagonists shift the equilibrium toward MRAP-independent receptor populations. Our HDX MS analysis shows that ACTH binding induces global stabilisation of MC2R and the MRAP N terminus, consistent with reinforcement of the receptor accessory protein interface. Antagonist binding by contrast destabilises this interface and increases dynamics in transmembrane helix 2 (TM2). Notably, TM2 destabilisation is retained when the MRAP complex is depleted. Together, MRAP association and palmitoylation define distinct MC2R assemblies with ligand-dependent dynamics, suggesting new ways to influence MC2R pharmacology.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Combines native MS and HDX MS to show that MC2R is palmitoylated when MRAP is depleted, and this modification shapes receptor conformation and antagonism.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该研究利用天然质谱和氢氘交换质谱揭示了黑皮质素2受体（MC2R）在MRAP蛋白缺失时发生广泛棕榈酰化修饰。这种翻译后修饰改变了受体构象，限制了ACTH结合，并影响拮抗剂的作用。研究为棕榈酰化在GPCR调控中的作用提供了机制见解，可应用于卵巢衰老等疾病模型。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 该研究利用天然质谱和氢氘交换质谱揭示了黑皮质素2受体（MC2R）在MRAP蛋白缺失时发生广泛棕榈酰化修饰。这种翻译后修饰改变了受体构象，限制了ACTH结合，并影响拮抗剂的作用。研究为棕榈酰化在GPCR调控中的作用提供了机制见解，可应用于卵巢衰老等疾病模型。

deep_dive.title: A paradoxical impact of alcohol on sleep-memory coupling
deep_dive.abstract: Sleep serves a fundamental role in memory consolidation, but this can be influenced by physiological context. The latter is impacted profoundly by acute ethanol consumption, but whether intoxication affects the role of sleep in memory consolidation remains unexplored. Here, we demonstrate that acute ethanol inverts the canonical link between sleep and memory in Drosophila. Typically, satiated flies require sleep for appetitive memory consolidation, but starved flies that must forage for food switch to sleep-independent memory. Ethanol selectively impairs memory consolidation in satiated flies by causing a switch to sleep-independent memory, which then cannot be supported because of ethanol-induced sedation. Under these conditions, sleep deprivation rescues memory and requires the upregulation of neuropeptide F, which signals through NPF receptors on PPL1 dopaminergic neurons. We suggest that reward-seeking behaviors, induced by starvation or ethanol, invoke sleep-independent memory. However, ethanol has a paradoxical impact, wherein it induces a switch in memory pathways but concurrently suppresses consolidation through the preferred pathway, thereby causing sleep to become detrimental for memory consolidation.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Investigates alcohol's effect on sleep-memory coupling in Drosophila, addressing neurobiological mechanisms of sleep
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 研究酒精对果蝇睡眠-记忆耦合的影响，涉及睡眠神经生物学机制
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 研究酒精对果蝇睡眠-记忆耦合的影响，涉及睡眠神经生物学机制
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 研究酒精对果蝇睡眠-记忆耦合的影响，涉及睡眠神经生物学机制
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: This study shows that acute ethanol inverts the role of sleep in memory consolidation in Drosophila, switching from sleep-dependent to sleep-independent memory, revealing a neurobiological mechanism linking sleep and memory.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该研究以果蝇为模型，发现急性乙醇摄入会逆转睡眠与记忆巩固之间的典型关系。在饱食果蝇中，通常需要睡眠来巩固记忆；而乙醇促使它们转向不依赖睡眠的记忆模式，但由于乙醇诱导的镇静作用，这种模式无法正常运作，反而导致记忆受损。睡眠剥夺可以挽救记忆，表明乙醇重塑了睡眠的神经生物学功能。这一成果揭示了酒精如何通过改变睡眠机制影响认知。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 该研究以果蝇为模型，发现急性乙醇摄入会逆转睡眠与记忆巩固之间的典型关系。在饱食果蝇中，通常需要睡眠来巩固记忆；而乙醇促使它们转向不依赖睡眠的记忆模式，但由于乙醇诱导的镇静作用，这种模式无法正常运作，反而导致记忆受损。睡眠剥夺可以挽救记忆，表明乙醇重塑了睡眠的神经生物学功能。这一成果揭示了酒精如何通过改变睡眠机制影响认知。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: neurobiological mechanisms of sleep and wakefulness

deep_dive.title: EEGDash: An open-source platform for machine learning on public neurophysiological data
deep_dive.abstract: Public neurophysiological datasets are increasingly accessible but remain hard to reuse: turning one into a trained model still takes thousands of lines of code for download, loading, format repair, windowing, and evaluation, and a dataset that meets metadata standards can still fail to load. EEG-Dash is a software resource that catalogues 791 publicly archived recordings (39,778 participants, over 86,051 hours) spanning electroencephalography (EEG), magnetoencephalography (MEG), intracranial EEG (iEEG), electromyography (EMG), and functional near-infrared spectroscopy (fNIRS) from the OpenNeuro and NEMAR archives. It exposes each dataset as an importable, queryable class that preserves signal attributes and loads into machine-learning workflows without custom code, delegating signal handling to MNE-Python, windowing to Braindecode, and format compliance to the official Brain Imaging Data Structure (BIDS) validator. A metadata-first registry adds semantic search, a format-repair layer, automatic dataset-level tags drawn from each source publication, and a feature-extraction framework. The catalogue, with per-record loadability and compliance metadata, supports benchmarking, model development, and cross-dataset analysis.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: open-source platform for neurophysiological data; EEG; sleep neuroscience
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 神经生理数据开源平台；EEG；睡眠神经科学
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 神经生理数据开源平台；EEG；睡眠神经科学
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 神经生理数据开源平台；EEG；睡眠神经科学
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: EEGDash catalogs 791 public neurophysiological recordings (EEG, MEG, iEEG, etc.) and provides importable, queryable classes for machine learning.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该研究开发了EEGDash平台，整理了来自OpenNeuro和NEMAR的791个公开神经生理记录（涵盖EEG、MEG、iEEG等），并将其封装为可导入、可查询的类，直接用于机器学习。该平台极大降低了神经科学数据复用的门槛，为睡眠神经科学研究（如基于EEG的睡眠分期、脑电分析）提供了标准化数据基础设施。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 该研究开发了EEGDash平台，整理了来自OpenNeuro和NEMAR的791个公开神经生理记录（涵盖EEG、MEG、iEEG等），并将其封装为可导入、可查询的类，直接用于机器学习。该平台极大降低了神经科学数据复用的门槛，为睡眠神经科学研究（如基于EEG的睡眠分期、脑电分析）提供了标准化数据基础设施。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: Papers central to 研究睡眠神经科学领域的学术文章, especially work that connects or combines: advancements in sleep neuroscience research; neurobiological mechanisms of sleep and wakefulness; neural regulation of the sleep-wake cycle; brain circuits involved in sleep control; neural mechanisms of rapid eye movement sleep; neurobiology of non-rapid eye movement sleep; recent advances in sleep neuroscience research; neural mechanisms underlying sleep wake regulation; neurobiological basis of clinical sleep disorders.

quick_skim.title: A Kuramoto-von Mises Time Series Model for Probabilistic Modeling of Coupled Oscillators
quick_skim.abstract: A system of coupled oscillators provides a fundamental framework for modeling a wide range of physical and biological phenomena. In neuroscience, the central nervous system exhibits synchronized oscillatory activity with adjacent brain regions, giving rise to traveling wave dynamics for instance during sleep. Similarly, in the gastrointestinal system, neuromuscular cells coordinate their oscillations to generate propagating waves of slow wave activity. To estimate probability distributions of multivariate phase relationships, existing approaches typically rely on equilibrium thermodynamics, expressing the system in a Boltzmann form through a pairwise exponential family distribution. However, these assumptions are often violated in real-world systems, which are inherently dynamic and frequently transition between equilibrium and non-equilibrium regimes. To address this, we propose an efficient method for estimating the probability distribution of coupled oscillators that does not assume thermodynamic equilibrium. Using a Langevin dynamics-based construction, the approach enables accurate modeling even in non-equilibrium regimes. The maximum likelihood estimation method is shown to have a closed form algebraic solution in the high sampling rate regime, a condition commonly satisfied by modern data acquisition systems, which makes it readily applicable in practice. We demonstrate its robustness on simulated data, where it outperforms existing approaches in non-equilibrium settings, and further illustrate its utility for characterizing dynamic brain traveling waves in response to brain stimulation and in hypothesis testing within the context of electrophysiologic recordings of the human stomach.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: coupled oscillator model applicable to sleep traveling waves
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 应用于睡眠行波的耦合振荡器模型
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 应用于睡眠行波的耦合振荡器模型
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 应用于睡眠行波的耦合振荡器模型
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a probabilistic model for coupled oscillators that captures non-equilibrium dynamics, with applications to neural oscillations during sleep.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文提出了一种Kuramoto-von Mises时间序列模型，用于对耦合振荡器进行概率建模，突破了平衡热力学假设的限制。模型可应用于神经科学中的睡眠行波和胃肠慢波活动，为分析睡眠中脑内振荡的相位关系和动态提供了理论工具。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文提出了一种Kuramoto-von Mises时间序列模型，用于对耦合振荡器进行概率建模，突破了平衡热力学假设的限制。模型可应用于神经科学中的睡眠行波和胃肠慢波活动，为分析睡眠中脑内振荡的相位关系和动态提供了理论工具。

quick_skim.title: Better Adherence, Richer Context: A Field Evaluation of LLM-Powered Conversational Voice Diaries for Sleep
quick_skim.abstract: Sleep diaries are central to behavioral sleep medicine and cognitive behavioral therapy for insomnia, yet daily completion is difficult to sustain, and static forms often provide limited context for interpreting night-to-night sleep variation. We designed an LLM-powered conversational voice diary that delivers clinically grounded morning and evening sleep diary questions through proactive smart-speaker prompts, structured conversational intake, and adaptive follow-up dialogue. We evaluated the system in a four-week between-subjects field study with 30 university students, comparing it with a text-based mobile diary using matched diary items, reporting windows, and reminder intervals. Compared with the text-based diary, the conversational voice diary showed higher adherence and elicited more detailed contextual self-report about routines, stressors, environmental conditions, and other sleep-related factors. Participants also described the voice diary as easier to integrate into daily routines, despite longer perceived completion time. However, voice-based conversational intake produced lower completeness for some structured diary fields, revealing a trade-off between expressive richness and structured precision. These findings show both the promise and the challenge of using LLM-powered conversational voice assistants for longitudinal health self-report.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: LLM-powered conversational voice diary for sleep adherence
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 基于大语言模型的对话式语音睡眠日记改善依从性
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 基于大语言模型的对话式语音睡眠日记改善依从性
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 基于大语言模型的对话式语音睡眠日记改善依从性
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This paper evaluates an LLM-powered conversational voice diary for sleep, showing better adherence and richer context compared to text-based diaries in a four-week field study.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文设计了一种由大语言模型驱动的对话式语音睡眠日记，通过智能音箱主动提问和适应性对话收集早晚睡眠数据，并在30名大学生中进行了四周现场实验。与文本日记相比，该系统显著提高了填写依从性并提供了更丰富的睡眠背景信息，为睡眠评估和认知行为治疗提供了更好的工具。

quick_skim.title: Evaluation of EEG Foundation Models for Event-Based Burst-Suppression Detection in ICU
quick_skim.abstract: Burst suppression (BS) is a clinically relevant electroencephalographic (EEG) pattern used to monitor sedation depth and brain activity in critically ill patients, particularly during induced coma in Intensive Care Units (ICUs). Automatic burst detection remains challenging because BS patterns vary substantially between patients and annotated datasets are scarce. Recently, EEG Foundation Models (FMs) have shown promise across several downstream EEG applications, but their usefulness for BS detection remains unexplored. We present the first study to evaluate EEG FMs for burst detection in reduced-montage ICU EEG without patient-specific calibration. We compare REVE-base, LUNA-large and LuMamba-Tiny with an adaptive thresholding baseline and a task-specific EEGNet baseline. Additionally, we complement conventional EEG window-based classification with event-based burst detection evaluation. This helps assessing clinically whether burst episodes are correctly detected, reducing the impact of expected annotation variability. The best model, REVE-base, achieved the highest event-based F1-score ($0.868 \pm 0.167$) and reduced burst-per-minute error by 52.1% and 36.2% compared to EEGNet and adaptive thresholding respectively, supporting FMs for scalable EEG monitoring in ICU. Ablation experiments showed that full fine-tuning was the most effective adaptation strategy with respect to frozen-backbone training, two-step fine-tuning, and LoRA-based adaptation, improving event-based F1-score over frozen-backbone training by up to $+0.102$ for LUNA-large. With reduced labeled datasets, pretrained REVE-base outperformed random initialization by $+0.723$ event-based F1 points at 25% of the cohort, demonstrating the benefit of pretraining FM representations when adapted to burst detection with limited labeled data.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: EEG foundation models for burst-suppression detection in ICU sleep monitoring
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 在ICU睡眠监测中评估脑电图基础模型用于爆发抑制检测
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 在ICU睡眠监测中评估脑电图基础模型用于爆发抑制检测
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 在ICU睡眠监测中评估脑电图基础模型用于爆发抑制检测
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This study evaluates EEG foundation models for automatic burst-suppression detection in ICU patients, addressing challenges in variable patterns and scarce annotations.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本研究首次评估了EEG基础模型在ICU患者爆发抑制自动检测中的表现。针对不同患者间模式差异大和标注数据稀缺的挑战，比较了多种基础模型与自适应阈值基线。结果表明EEG基础模型在无患者特异性校准下仍能有效检测爆发抑制，为临床睡眠监测提供了新工具。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本研究首次评估了EEG基础模型在ICU患者爆发抑制自动检测中的表现。针对不同患者间模式差异大和标注数据稀缺的挑战，比较了多种基础模型与自适应阈值基线。结果表明EEG基础模型在无患者特异性校准下仍能有效检测爆发抑制，为临床睡眠监测提供了新工具。
